body.title: [triage] Capability and token budget are two axes — the frontier ranks were measured at an uncontrolled max_tokens
body.description:
**⛔ FAIL — KICKED BACK TO DESIGN 2026-07-13 (Akien: 'those should get sent back to the design step as FAIL for learning from and fixing').**

**THIS TICKET ENTERED SPRINT WITH NO INTENTION.** Under D-intentions-are-the-root-2026-07-13 and proof-on-close, THE INTENTION IS THE PROPERTY MUTATION-RED BREAKS. With no intention there is **no property to break**, so this ticket **cannot be proven as it stands** — it could only ever close hollow, or `shipped-unproven` for a reason that is not the real one.

**IT IS NOT DEFECTIVE WORK — IT IS INCOMPLETE DESIGN.** So the honest disposition is BACK TO DESIGN, not a backfilled intention. CC drafting the missing intention would have **papered over the real defect**: 150 tickets cleared the design step without an intention, which is a **gate hole**, not 150 individual oversights. Per CLAUDE.md, the nuisance stays VISIBLE so it drives finding the fix — never papered over, which sucks silently forever (CP4).

**CP2 — FAIL = FURTHER ADVANCE IN LEARNING.** This kickback IS the learning signal. The fix is upstream: the triage→sprint transition must reject a missing, empty, or degenerate (`TBD`/`unknown`) intention (T-sprint-tickets-with-no-intention-cannot-be-proven).

**TO RE-ENTER SPRINT:** state the intention as a present-tense contract — *'I intend that X, because Y'* — where X names a property a hollow build would violate.

**AND NOTE WHO FAILED HERE.** Akien wrote none of these tickets. **CC wrote all 149.** So 'the design step that failed' is CC, and the honest rule is not 'a human must declare it' — it is: **THE AUTHOR DECLARES THE INTENTION AT AUTHORING TIME, FROM THE CONVERSATION THAT PRODUCED THE TICKET.** That is what makes it `declared`: not that a human typed it (a ratified string and a machine-written string are byte-identical), but that it was stated from evidence INDEPENDENT of the artifact — the design conversation, which traces to Akien's intent. An intention RECONSTRUCTED LATER FROM THE TICKET BODY has no independent evidence and is `extracted-from-artifact`: a description, never a spec (D-provenance-is-the-safety-property-2026-07-13). **The failure was not forgetting a field. It was filing 149 tickets without ever pausing to say what I intended by them.**

---

Akien, 2026-07-09: 'we're starting with easy, and moving toward hard. so max tokens might need to be silly for early testing, and then reducing it iteratively to sort those limits for real?'

That is the right cut, and it invalidates part of what shipped in T-inference-cost-first-sort-strands-cloud-fleet.

TWO AXES, currently conflated by escalation_matrix:
  CAPABILITY — can the model reach the right answer AT ALL? Must be measured with a budget so generous it cannot bind. Otherwise a wrong answer is ambiguous between 'cannot' and 'ran out of room'.
  BUDGET — how many tokens does it NEED? A cost property, and the one routing actually spends (InferenceRequest.max_tokens). Found by walking max_tokens DOWN until the model stops passing. That floor is per-model, per-band data.

EVIDENCE THIS IS NOT THEORETICAL:
- gemini-2.5-flash was first scored 2/4 on b5_frontier, i.e. LESS capable than a local 32b. Its replies were truncated (finish_reason='max_tokens', a spelling the instrument did not recognise). Fixed; re-measured 3/4 + 1 TRUNC.
- deepseek-r1:32b was recorded as FAILING b5-frobenius (answered 23, truth 43) at max_tokens=4096, finish_reason=stop.
- deepseek-r1:14b then PASSED b5-frobenius at max_tokens=8192, live, at hop 0.
  A 14b passing what a 32b 'failed' is either a real non-monotonicity or a budget artifact. The two runs used DIFFERENT BUDGETS, so as measured they are not comparable, and no conclusion is licensed.

WHAT THIS THREATENS IN THE SHIPPED CHANGE:
- The registry now records capability_evidence='measured:<note>' for llama3.2:3b, deepseek-r1:14b, deepseek-r1:32b and deepseek-v3.1:671b-cloud. Those measurements did NOT control max_tokens (4096 for the b5 band, matrix default at the time). The EVIDENCE POINTER IS THEREFORE WEAKER THAN THE WORD 'measured' IMPLIES.
- What survives: deepseek-v3.1:671b-cloud cleared b5 4/4 at the SAME budget where the 32b failed one query, so 'the 671b is at least as capable as the 32b, at 4096 tokens' stands. The resolver-level proof (escalation resolves to a non-owned_local source) is structural and does not depend on any of this.
- What does NOT survive: 'deepseek-r1:32b is measurably not frontier-capable' as an unconditional statement, and the implied total order 14b < 32b < 671b.

WORK:
1. escalation_matrix: make max_tokens a swept AXIS, not a flag. Two phases:
   (a) CAPABILITY PASS at a ceiling budget high enough that no model truncates (start 32768; raise until TRUNC count is zero, and RECORD the ceiling used).
   (b) BUDGET WALK: for every (model, query) that passed, step max_tokens down a ladder (e.g. 32k,16k,8k,4k,2k,1k) and record the smallest budget that still passes — the token floor.
2. Record BOTH in the note, with conditions attached (budget, temperature, sample count, date). A result at 4096 is a result about the model AT 4096 (see memory: epistemology-experiment-only).
3. Sample count > 1 per (model, query, budget). One sample at temperature 0 is one sample, not a property.
4. Re-derive capability_evidence from the CEILING pass only. Restate or drop any claim the new data does not support — annotate, never silently overwrite (CP2).
5. Feed the token floor into routing: max_tokens is a cost lever the router can set per (model, difficulty), instead of a constant that silently turns a capable model into a failing one.

RELATED: T-escalation-truncation-misclassified-as-capability — the domain still classifies a truncation as CAPABILITY and spends up a tier for want of tokens. That ticket is the runtime half of this one; this ticket is the measurement half.

**Test plan:** red->green, hermetic. RED: a capability record exists whose evidence does not name the budget it was measured at. GREEN: every 'measured:' evidence string names its ceiling budget and sample count, and a guard test rejects any capability claim recorded at a budget where that model truncated. The live re-measurement is DATA in the memory store, never a green assertion.

**Decision:** decision:D-domains-general-with-device-owned-specializations-2026-07-08

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.fail_reason: entered sprint with no intention — cannot be proven; design incomplete
